'''Alex Mellor''' (born 24 September 1994) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward or {{rlp|ce}} for and [[Captain (rugby league)|captains]] the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He has previously played for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and for [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Mellor was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England. Mellor is a talented cricketer and represented Yorkshire schoolboys until age 15, when rugby league became his chosen career.{{citation needed|date=November 2025}}

== Playing career ==

=== Bradford Bulls ===
Mellor had been involved in the [[Bradford Bulls]] scholarship system from under-15s.

In the 2013 season, he featured in round 20 [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] and round 27 against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].

In the 2014 season, Mellor featured in the pre-season game against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. He featured in round 10 [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] to round 13 [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] then in round 21 against Huddersfield to round 23 [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. He played in round 27 [[London Broncos]]. He signed a new one-year deal with the Bradford outfit despite their relegation to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

In the 2015 season, Mellor featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Castleford Tigers]] and Leeds. He featured in round 1 ([[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]) to round 4 ([[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]]) then in round 9 ([[London Broncos]]) to round 16 ([[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]). Mellor played in the qualifier 3 against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] to qualifier 4 against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] then in qualifier 7 [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. Mellor played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in round 5 [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Mid-way through the season, Mellor signed a two-year extension to his contract.{{citation needed|date=October 2017}}

In the 2016 season, Mellor featured in the pre-season friendlies against Leeds and Castleford. He featured in round 2 ([[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]) to round 3 [[Swinton Lions]] then in round 5 [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] to round 7 [[London Broncos]]. He played in round 10 ([[Dewsbury Rams]]) to round 13 against Swinton then in round 15 against Leigh. Mellor featured in round 17 ([[Workington Town]]) to round 21 [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] then in round 23 [[Featherstone Rovers]]. Mellor featured in the Championship Shield game 1 [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] to the final against [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]]. Mellor played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the 4th round against the Dewsbury club. 

=== Huddersfield Giants ===
Following Bradford's liquidation before the 2017 season, he signed a three-year deal with [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15004673.UPDATED__Mellor_and_Welham_latest_to_move_on_from_liquidated_Bulls|title=UPDATED: Mellor and Welham latest to move on from liquidated Bulls|work=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=5 January 2017|accessdate=31 January 2017}}</ref>

Mellor featured in round 1 against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] to round 6 against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].

=== Leeds Rhinos ===
On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup final]] victory for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 21 June 2022, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced that Mellor would join the club with immediate effect, having signed a two-and-a-half-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 June 2022 |title=Alex Mellor: Castleford Tigers sign Leeds Rhinos back-rower |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61889169 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He made his Tigers debut later that week, in a [[Golden point#Rugby league|golden-point]] win against [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wheldon Road]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=26 June 2022 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 17-16 Catalans Dragons - Danny Richardson kicks winner |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61884601 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Heppenstall |first=Ross |date=27 June 2022 |title=Alex Mellor out to silence Leeds Rhinos critics after Castleford Tigers move |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/alex-mellor-castleford-tigers-leeds-24332621 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> He scored his first try for the club against his former side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] on 19 August.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 August 2022 |title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants 36-10 Castleford Tigers - Giants' top-two hopes boosted |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62582761 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Ahead of the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]], Mellor was assigned squad number 12 and also appointed Castleford vice-captain.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=14 February 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm Paul McShane and Alex Mellor as captain and vice-captain for 2023 |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-confirm-paul-mcshane-and-alex-mellor-as-captain-and-vice-captain-for-2023-4027427 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 January 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers 2023 squad numbers: Gareth Widdop to wear No. 6 shirt |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2023-squad-numbers |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On 12 May, playing as a centre, he scored two tries for Castleford in their 22–46 loss against Catalans Dragons.<ref>{{Cite web |date=12 May 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 22-46 Catalans Dragons - Tigers suffer fourth straight loss |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65545804 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Three weeks later, at the [[Magic Weekend#2023: Newcastle|2023 Magic Weekend]], he scored two tries in Castleford's 26–24 win against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bazeley |first=Marc |date=3 June 2023 |title=Leeds Rhinos 24-26 Castleford Tigers: Jason Qareqare's converted try snatches vital Magic Weekend win |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-castleford/58333 |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> Mellor made twenty-six appearances throughout the season as the club narrowly avoided relegation by finishing above [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]].<ref name="LRL 2023 review">{{cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=22 September 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/ |access-date=11 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He said, "We've massively underperformed as a team," and that looking forward, "There's a lot of improvements that need to happen across the board."<ref name="LRL 2023 review" />

Mellor suffered a knee injury in a friendly against [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]], forcing him to miss the opening two rounds.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=17 January 2024 |title=Alex Mellor to miss start of Super League season in injury blow for Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/alex-mellor-to-miss-start-of-super-league-season-in-injury-blow-for-castleford-tigers-4482575 |access-date=19 July 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He displayed impressive form as the season progressed with Castleford director of rugby Danny Wilson praising his "tough line-running",<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=21 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' 2025 squad continues to take shape with Alex Mellor re-signing |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-2025-squad-continues-to-take-shape-with-alex-mellor-re-signing-4637657 |access-date=19 July 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> and subsequently signed a two-year contract extension in May.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 May 2024 |title=Castleford's Mellor and Robb sign two-year deals |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp00l4d3ev3o |access-date=19 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 12 July, Mellor scored two tries in the Tigers' round 17 win against [[London Broncos|London]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=12 July 2024 |title=Controlled Castleford beat bottom club Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c99wgzeeelwo |access-date=19 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> At the end of the season, he was awarded Castleford Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=21 September 2024 |title=Every Super League club's Player of the Year: Mikey Lewis, Rhyse Martin, Matt Dufty... |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/every-super-league-clubs-player-of-the-year-mikey-lewis-rhyse-martin-matt-dufty |access-date=24 January 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

While Castleford finished 11th in the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]], Mellor's performances were praised by ''[[The Yorkshire Post]]'' which described him as "a shining light in the dark times" and "reliable, wholehearted and prepared to put his body on the line".<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=5 September 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers 26 Wakefield Trinity 22: Old magic returns to Wheldon Road as hosts deal hammer blow to rivals' play-off hopes |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-26-wakefield-trinity-22-old-magic-returns-to-wheldon-road-as-hosts-deal-hammer-blow-to-rivals-play-off-hopes-5305721 |access-date=24 January 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref name="TYP Dec 2025">{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=2 December 2025 |title=Alex Mellor sees light at end of tunnel for Castleford Tigers amid genuine 2026 optimism |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/alex-mellor-sees-light-at-end-of-tunnel-for-castleford-tigers-amid-genuine-2026-optimism-5424747 |access-date=4 June 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He played twenty-six matches, registering the most minutes of any forward and topping metres gained for the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |date=13 November 2025 |title=2025 Season Review: Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/5578/2025-season-review-castleford-tigers |access-date=4 June 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref> In May, he signed a contract extension until the end of 2027; director of rugby [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]] said, "He's been our most consistent player in the side this season and has shown extraordinary leadership on and off the field."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=23 May 2025 |title=Castleford start retention drive with key player signing long-term deal |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-start-retention-drive-with-key-player-signing-long-term-deal |access-date=24 January 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He retained the Castleford Player of the Year award, as well as being named Players' Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=15 November 2025 |title=Super League 2025: Castleford Tigers season review |url=https://www.totalrl.com/super-league-2025-castleford-tigers-season-review/ |url-status=live |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20251208232048/https://www.totalrl.com/super-league-2025-castleford-tigers-season-review/ |archive-date=8 December 2025 |access-date=24 January 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

Mellor was appointed club captain for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]] by new head coach [[Ryan Carr (rugby league)|Ryan Carr]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=24 January 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers appoint new captain as Ryan Carr era begins |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-appoint-new-captain-as-ryan-carr-era-begins |access-date=24 January 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> and spoke of his optimism following improvements around the club.<ref name="TYP Dec 2025" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=23 December 2025 |title=Alex Mellor on 'different feel' to Castleford Tigers ahead of new season |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/alex-mellor-on-different-feel-to-castleford-tigers-ahead-of-new-season-5446882 |access-date=4 June 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He sustained a broken leg in round 2, however this didn't require surgery and he returned after five weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |date=26 February 2026 |title=Castleford captain Mellor out with broken leg |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cdr26v77jz2o |access-date=4 June 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers captain Alex Mellor set for long absence with horror injury |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-captain-alex-mellor-set-for-long-absence-with-horror-injury |access-date=4 June 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He was named in the team of the week in rounds 11 and 12 consecutively for his performances in Castleford's victories against [[York Knights]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 May 2026 |title=Round 11 Team of the Week |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/6087/round-11-team-of-the-week |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2058903412218167447 |user=SuperLeague |title=The Round 12 #SuperLeague Team of the Week |author=Super League |author-link=Super League |date=25 May 2026 |access-date=31 May 2026}}</ref>
